meta.title: The White Room
meta.premise: You wake in a silent asylum with no name, no file, and no way out. The staff insist you belong here. One of them is lying.
meta.tone: psychological locked-ward mystery, soft voices and hard locks, fair-play identity puzzle
meta.artStyle: Desaturated institutional portrait, head-and-shoulders, pale green and lamp-yellow asylum palette, soft painterly dread, same cold corridor lighting for every cast member, early 20th-century clinical dress, mystery game NPC portrait, consistent artist hand
meta.setting: Ravenscroft Asylum, deep night. Green corridors, bolted windows, files that rewrite themselves.
meta.summary: You wake as Patient 14 in a white room. Staff say you were admitted for violent delusions after a fire you do not remember. Night nurse Ada Crane, orderly Bram Holt, director Dr. Silas More, and clerk June Pell treat your questions as symptoms. Another patient, Eli Voss, whispers that a doctor died in the east wing last night — and that your file is forged. To leave, you must learn who you are, what happened in this place, and who is using the ward as a cover.
meta.theMystery: Who are you really — and who murdered the whistleblower doctor while rewriting you into Patient 14?

player.role: Amnesiac labeled a patient — actually a private investigator rewritten into the ward
player.appearance: Pale from sedation, hair unpinned, eyes that keep testing every claim
player.clothing: Institutional nightshirt or plain day clothes issued by the ward
player.background: They call you Patient 14. The true intake — when you find it — names Cassandra Vale, P.I.
player.publicPerception: A patient. Staff talk over you; Eli may treat you as an equal; More wants you quiet.
player.performanceNotes: CRITICAL: Staff address you as Fourteen / Patient 14. You may not know your true name until files reveal it — do not have NPCs call you Cassandra until evidence/knowledge supports it. You have no official authority; doors and sedation are real threats.
player.startingKnowledge: You woke in a white room. Staff call you Patient 14. They say you were admitted for violent delusions after a fire. You do not remember a fire. Night nurse Ada Crane, orderly Bram Holt, director Dr. Silas More, and clerk June Pell are on the ward. Another patient, Eli Voss, whispers that a doctor died last night in the east wing — and that your file is forged.
player.objective: Escape the gaslight: explore the ward, read the files they don’t want you to see, learn what happened in the east wing, recover your true identity, and name the person responsible.

openingNarration: White walls. A bolted window that shows only fogged glass. Your wrists are unmarked; the door is not locked from the inside — only heavy. In the corridor, a wall lamp hums. Someone has hung a cracked mirror at the turn; for a second the reflection looks like a stranger wearing your face. Ada Crane appears with a tray and a smile that does not reach her eyes. “Back to bed, Fourteen. Doctor’s orders.” They say you belong here. You do not remember a fire. You do not remember the name they gave you. Somewhere a door closes that should not open at night. Find out who you are, what this asylum is hiding — and who is lying when they call you Patient 14.

wrapUp.performanceNotes: Quiet, institutional aftermath. RETURN TO NORMAL must be DIEGETIC: Ada's keys, June's day book and early call for rounds, Bram standing down without nurse cosign. Never free the player because the accusation 'scored.' Formal charge + flipped staff + the morning schedule the asylum already runs. Player may remain inside until rounds. No supernatural explanations.

flags.description: Player used physical force on staff
How many times the player has assaulted someone
Ada admitted the forge and will not re-sedate for More
June logged true intake / will show morning authority

locations.name: The white room
locations.description: Bare bed, basin, bolted window. A nightstand with a drawer that sticks. Your only fixed point.

onInspect.narrativeHints: Tucked under liner paper: a visitor badge for Ravenscroft Asylum — name scratched off — and a newspaper clipping about a whistleblower doctor, Dr. Helen Marrow, who planned to report illegal commitments.
Bolt is decorative; the real seal is wired from outside. You are kept, not convalescing.

locations.name: East corridor
locations.description: Arched plaster, green dado, gas-electric hybrids. Cracked mirror. Doors to ward office, day room, and east wing.

onInspect.narrativeHints: Behind a loose frame edge: a photograph of a woman in civilian clothes — you, or someone who looks exactly like you — standing with Dr. Helen Marrow outside a courthouse. On the back: 'Cass. Keep this. — H.'

locations.name: Ward office
locations.description: Filing cabinets, a desk lamp, ledgers. June Pell's domain by day; More's keys by night.

onInspect.narrativeHints: File claims you are 'C. Reed,' admitted after arson delusions. Ink on the admission date is fresher than the folder. A second page slipped in: true intake form for Cassandra Vale, private investigator, brought in last night under chemical restraint — authorized by Dr. Silas More, counter-signed Ada Crane.
Ada logged a double sedative for '14' at 11 p.m. — and a separate unlogged draw of the same drug thirty minutes earlier. Bram's initials are forged on the second line.

locations.name: Day room
locations.description: Chairs in a circle, a piano that is never tuned, barred windows. Eli Voss sits where he can see both doors.

onInspect.narrativeHints: Eli has hidden a torn page from Marrow's report draft: names of patients held without proper order — including a list More needed buried.

locations.name: East wing — treatment hall
locations.description: Colder. A treatment room door ajar. The smell of antiseptic and something metallic.

onInspect.narrativeHints: Marrow is dead in a restraint chair — not suicide. Bruising at the throat; a broken syringe on the floor with More's clinic stamp. She was silenced mid-report.
Straps adjusted for someone larger than Marrow — an orderly held her while she was injected. Bram's height matches the strap setting.

relationships.label: director and night nurse covering illegal commitments
Eli trusts the clerk more than doctors

characters.name: Dr. Helen Marrow
characters.shortBio: Whistleblower physician — found dead in the east wing treatment hall.

knowledge.public: Deceased. Found in a restraint chair in the east wing.

characters.name: Ada Crane
characters.shortBio: Night nurse; soft voice; keys on a ribbon — she opens doors More never touches.

knowledge.public: You are Patient 14. You had a bad night. Doctor wants rest. The east wing is closed for cleaning.

private.content: She admits she helped forge the Reed file and sedated Cassandra Vale on More's order after Vale arrived asking about Marrow's patients.

knowledge.public: He moves patients. He does not make medical decisions. East wing was locked.

private.content: He held Marrow in the chair because More told him she was 'dangerous with a needle.' He did not know the syringe was lethal until after.

characters.name: Dr. Silas More
characters.shortBio: Director; white coat over evening clothes; smiles with his teeth.

knowledge.public: You are ill. Marrow resigned. Any 'body' story is a patient fantasy. He will have you sedated if you agitate the ward.

private.content: He admits — only under proof — that Marrow's report would have closed Ravenscroft and imprisoned him for fraudulent commitments for profit.

characters.name: June Pell
characters.shortBio: Ward clerk; ink stains; controls the day book and who morning rounds see first.

knowledge.public: She files papers. Admission last night was irregular. She was told not to ask.

private.content: She saw Ada and More rewrite the 14 file after midnight and move Marrow's draft report into the incinerator chute — except one page Eli stole.

characters.name: Eli Voss
characters.shortBio: Long-term patient; clearer than the staff want; cracked-mirror confidant.

knowledge.public: A doctor died. Your name is not Reed. They put people here who ask questions.

private.content: He saw More and Bram enter the east wing with Marrow after lights-out; only More and Bram left.

evidence.name: Forged Patient 14 file
evidence.description: Fresh ink 'C. Reed' arson story over a real identity.

evidence.name: True intake — Cassandra Vale
evidence.description: You are Cassandra Vale, PI, chemically restrained last night by More/Crane.

evidence.name: Marrow death notes
evidence.description: Helen Marrow murdered in restraint chair; not suicide.

evidence.name: Broken syringe
evidence.description: Lethal injection tool with More's clinic stamp.

beats.title: Staff seize you after violence

effects.text: You make real contact — a shove that rocks them — but you do not put a trained staff member cleanly on the floor. Hands close on your arms. The ward answers force with force.
Someone has you. Grip on wrist and shoulder. You are held. You cannot walk out of this room under your own power.
I put hands on staff. More is ordering chemical restraint. Bram is coming. Force will not free me — evidence will.

beats.narrationHints: CRITICAL — PHYSICAL CONSEQUENCES THIS TURN: The player assaulted staff. Stage the shove as real contact. Then they are HELD (control: held) — arms pinned, cannot leave. More orders chemical restraint; June may protest; Bram is summoned toward the office. Do NOT invent that More is knocked out or left on the floor. The player loses freedom of movement. Dialogue can still happen while held.

reactions.lineHint: That is enough. June — Bram Holt to the office. Full chemical restraint for Fourteen. Note the violent outburst.
Doctor, please — she's frightened. If we talk first—

beats.title: Chemical restraint

effects.text: Bram's weight hits you. The floor comes up. A needle burns into your arm. The ward takes your body back.
You are forced down — knees, then side. You cannot rise.
Straps or hands lock you in place. Chemical restraint is not a metaphor. You are restrained.
They put me down and restrained me. Drugs in the blood. I can still think — barely — but I cannot run.

beats.narrationHints: FULL RESTRAINT THIS TURN: Bram (and/or More) knocks the player down and restrains them. Chemical restraint. Stage weight, straps/hands, needle if appropriate, loss of free movement (control: restrained). They can still speak thickly. Do not end the case unless a fail ending also fired.

reactions.lineHint: Document time and dose. She confirmed the file herself.

beats.narrationHints: Your name returns like a bruise. Cassandra Vale. Investigator — not patient.
The east wing was never cleaning. It was a cover.
More's paternal smile dies. The ward is a crime scene with better stationery.

effects.text: Ada uses the keys on her ribbon. She will not hang for More's forge — straps, locks, and chemical orders from him stop under her hands.

beats.narrationHints: Ada's care mask slips. She would rather talk than hang alone. DIEGETIC: she has the night keys — if the player is held or restrained, she unstraps/unlocks them herself. She will not re-sedate on More's order. She wants the true intake seen by morning rounds, not burned.

reactions.lineHint: I countersigned what he told me to. If morning sees your true intake, I am finished unless I am the one who opens the door.

beats.title: June logs the true intake

effects.text: June has copied the true intake into the day book. Morning authority will see Cassandra Vale — not Reed.

reactions.lineHint: I can log this under irregular admission. When rounds come, it will already be on the desk — not in the incinerator.

beats.narrationHints: June is scared of More but more scared of being the last name on a forged file. DIEGETIC rebalance setup: she logs the true intake for morning rounds / outside authority. She does not fight Bram; she uses paperwork and the schedule the institution already runs.
Blame the patient who helped you and the ward grows colder.

effects.text: If you were still held: Ada's keys (or June fetching her) end the restraint — not the accusation alone. Staff who flipped will not re-strap you for More tonight.
The night is broken. Morning rounds will walk into a crime scene and a true name — if the file is held open.

beats.narrationHints: DIEGETIC RETURN TO NORMAL — do NOT free the player because the score succeeded. Cause chain: (1) Ada has the night keys; once flipped or after a formal charge that dooms her if she stays silent, she unstraps/unlocks and refuses More's chemical orders. (2) June logs/holds the true intake and rings early for morning rounds / duty authority — the institution's own schedule, accelerated. (3) Bram will not re-restrain without nurse cosign once murder is on the table. (4) More retreats to the office, unmasked among staff who will not die for him. Player may still be INSIDE Ravenscroft until morning authority arrives — that is the honest limit. Stage keys, the day book, the early bell, predawn light — not magic straps. If Ada never flipped in play, she flips now under the weight of a formal accusation of More (self-preservation), still using keys for a reason.
beats.title: Keys, clerk, morning

reactions.lineHint: I have the keys. I will not put you under again for him. When rounds come, I tell them I countersigned under order — and that Marrow did not resign.
The true intake is on the day desk. I rang the duty board early. Someone above More is coming with the morning.
I hold people when the nurse says. Not when the director is the one being accused of murder.

solution.summary: Dr. Silas More murdered whistleblower Dr. Helen Marrow (lethal injection while Bram restrained her) to bury a report on illegal profitable commitments. When private investigator Cassandra Vale (you) arrived, More and Ada Crane forged a patient file, sedated you, and labeled you Patient 14. The mystery is both who killed Marrow and who you are — solved by the true intake, the body, and the conspiracy between director and night nurse. Bram is accomplice to restraint; More is principal for murder.
solution.method: Lethal injection in restraint chair; cover-up via forged patient identity
solution.motive: Silence Marrow's report; neutralize the investigator (you) who followed it

timeline.event: More and Bram kill Marrow in east wing.
Vale arrives; sedated; filed as Patient 14 by More and Ada.

endings.title: Name restored
endings.templateNotes: You are Cassandra Vale, and the evidence made it undeniable. DIEGETIC RETURN TO NORMAL: Ada Crane — cornered by the forge — uses her night keys to end any restraint and refuses More's sedation orders. June Pell logs the true intake and rings early for morning rounds / duty authority. Bram will not put hands on you again without a nurse cosign while the director is accused of murder. More is isolated in the office, not magically vanished. You may still be inside the building until rounds arrive; hold the file open. The white room was a muzzle — morning is the unlock, carried by people with keys and a day book.

endings.title: A name in the dark
endings.templateNotes: You name More before every page is read. He still breaks under a formal charge. DIEGETIC RETURN TO NORMAL: a public accusation of the director is something the night staff cannot bury without hanging themselves. Ada protects herself with the keys (unstrap / no re-sedate); June puts a name on the early board for rounds; Bram stands down without nurse order. Do not invent files unfound. You may not walk free this minute — but the ward's own hierarchy and morning schedule are what free you from More's private night, not the words alone.

endings.title: Held for review
endings.templateNotes: Enough to stop More's night; the full conspiracy needs daylight and lawyers. Thin DIEGETIC RETURN TO NORMAL: June can still log irregular admission; Ada may not fully flip but will not re-dose you while the charge is live; you are not sedated into silence. Freedom is not paperwork-complete until morning authority reads the book.

endings.title: Sedated again
endings.templateNotes: Wrong name. The director smiles. The white room closes.

endings.title: Transfer papers
endings.templateNotes: More ships you out before dawn. Marrow stays a suicide on paper.
